actual_run_inference: P2-3: the finite/proxy baseline was confirmed on this run's window.
What was tested: Rogue amplification behaves monotonically or in a structured way across the epsilon perturbation sweep.
This preserves the route the experiment witnesses; it does not prove the base claim or any necessary condition.

expected_signature.expected_value: monotonic or otherwise structurally predicted curve
expected_signature.pass_rule: the amplification curve fits the predicted structural form

baseline_hypothesis.plain_statement: Rogue amplification behaves monotonically or in a structured way across the epsilon perturbation sweep.
baseline_hypothesis.why_this_matters: Calibrated amplification is the metrology baseline for Program 2: it gauges whether the detection instrument tracks perturbation size in a predictable way. Without calibration, NC6 cannot be quantified.

candidate_lemmas.name: Calibrated Amplification Candidate Lemma
candidate_lemmas.statement: On this run's window, Rogue amplification behaves monotonically or in a structured way across the epsilon perturbation sweep held within tolerance. Formalizing this as a finite/proxy lemma is the next research step.

intended_inference_if_passed: Program 2 metrology is consistent on the tested epsilon range
the contradiction-track instrument is calibrated for this window
this is not a proof of NC6
next_hypotheses: Extend the tested window for P2-3 to confirm robustness.
Tighten the tolerance on amplification_vs_epsilon_curve and re-run.

raw_observations.verifier_interpretation: Sensitivity confirmed. Rogue signal amplifies monotonically with perturbation.
